item.title: KPMG and Harvey Nichols among employers found to have paid less than UK minimum wage
item.description: Government steps up its enforcement of newly strengthened workers’ rights

item.title: AI is moving from answering questions to taking action
item.description: China is likely to be both the testing ground and a leader in the use of agents

item.title: UK to protect steel industry with new import tariffs
item.description: Move brings Britain in line with US, EU and Canada which have sought to bolster domestic producers

item.title: FirstFT: Pakistan and Afghanistan pause hostilities after Kabul attack
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Iran inflicts ‘extensive’ damage on Qatar LNG facility, and Fed chief says oil crisis will worsen US inflation

item.title: Russia considers sending armed naval patrols to protect ‘shadow fleet’
item.description: Putin ally Patrushev says Moscow could deploy ‘mobile firing groups’ after series of suspected Ukrainian attacks on ships

item.title: UK to procure more anti-drone missiles in show of support for Gulf allies
item.description: LMMs have proven effective in countering strikes by Iran and its proxies in the region

item.title: Unilever and Kraft Heinz held talks over food merger uniting ketchup and mayo
item.description: Exploration of deal worth tens of billions of dollars reflects struggle of both companies to combat subdued demand

item.title: Lex Greensill fails to persuade court to throw out UK director ban case
item.description: Australian financier must go to trial over fate after Insolvency Service launched probe

item.title: US relaxes sanctions on Venezuela’s PDVSA amid global energy squeeze
item.description: Licence allows nation’s state oil group to sell directly to American companies and on international market

item.title: BofA pitches bets against European private credit
item.description: US bank told clients that European stocks exposed to private credit had 30 per cent ‘downside risk’

item.title: Silver linings to the heavy cloud of higher energy prices
item.description: Natural gas consumption has fallen and resilience has improved across Europe and in the UK

item.title: Nintendo wins by not playing the power game
item.description: The company’s exclusive titles have kept gamers loyal without chasing technological superiority

item.title: How hard will war hit the Gulf’s economies?
item.description: The highly globalised UAE has felt the impact of conflict more than insular Saudi Arabia

item.title: French music streamer Deezer hit by deluge of AI fraud
item.description: Industry under threat from fraudsters uploading and repeatedly playing tracks created by AI to extract royalties

item.title: Walmart wins patents to give algorithms more sway over prices
item.description: Move comes amid intense debate in the US about using automated systems to adjust costs for consumers

item.title: The oil market mess is much worse than you think
item.description: Oh, man . . .

item.title: Two shipments of Russian oil and gas head to Cuba in defiance of US
item.description: First cargo on course to arrive on Monday as Donald Trump taunts communist island while administration engages in talks

item.title: Game bird shooting in England faces stricter regulation
item.description: Government seeks to tighten rules governing the release of pheasants, grouse and partridges into the wild

item.title: Ingredients for a successful LBO: lots of equity, and some Trump stardust
item.description: The financing of Electronic Arts is the biggest of its kind since the financial crisis

item.title: Ithaca says policy clarity puts Cambo oil and gas project closer to approval
item.description: Company suggests certainty over future tax regime is helping unlock one of UK North Sea’s biggest developments

item.title: Iran vows to retaliate against Gulf energy after largest gasfield hit
item.description: South Pars provides most of Iran’s domestic gas and is critical to its electricity generation

item.title: Green Party leader pledges £8.4bn to stop UK energy bills rising
item.description: Zack Polanski says party would prevent increases in household energy costs through higher taxes on capital gains

item.title: Economists raise UK inflation forecasts after energy costs surge
item.description: Bank of England now expected to keep interest rates on hold at its meeting on Thursday

item.title: MFS owner Paresh Raja hit with worldwide freezing order
item.description: Businessman barred from spending more than £5,000 a week without administrators’ consent

item.title: No FT, no alpha
item.description: Your most reliable source for US biotech M&A, apparently

item.title: How can the UK shield households from higher energy bills?
item.description: Keir Starmer has made clear the government is unlikely to intervene at scale until higher prices hit consumers

item.title: Kirkland defies private equity downturn with record $11mn partner pay
item.description: Chicago-founded law firm becomes first to break $10bn barrier for annual revenues

item.title: Can AI help me find a home?
item.description: New technology is transforming house-hunting — but will Machine vision and Gaussian splats replace portals and estate agents?

item.title: Trump’s war will boost the clean energy sector he despises
item.description: Turmoil in oil and gas markets is pushing governments to seek alternatives

item.title: Should AI companies have the power to decide if chatbots live or die?
item.description: The final episode of our podcast series examines whether can AI help us cheat death, and if chatbots have a lifespan too
